back_translation: Samuel said to all Israel, 'Behold, I have listened to your voice in all that you have said to me, and I have set a king over you.'
And now, behold, the king is walking before you, and I am old and gray-headed, and my sons are with you, and I have been walking before you from my youth until this day.

Here I am. Bear witness against me before the LORD and before His anointed one — whose ox have I taken, whose donkey have I taken, whom have I cheated, whom have I oppressed, from whose hand have I received a bribe in order to close my eyes against what is wrong? Tell me, and I will restore it to you.
And they answered, 'You have not cheated us, and you have not oppressed us, and you have not taken anything from the hand of anyone.'
He said to them, 'The LORD is witness against you, and His anointed one is witness today, that you have not found anything in my hand.' And they answered, 'He is witness.'
Samuel said to the people, 'The LORD is the One who appointed Moses and Aaron, and who brought your ancestors up from the land of Egypt.
And now, take your stand, and I will judge a case with you before the face of the LORD, together with all the righteous deeds of the LORD that He has done for you and for your ancestors.
When Jacob came to Egypt, your ancestors cried out to the LORD, and the LORD sent Moses and Aaron, and they brought your ancestors out from Egypt, and He settled them in this place.

But they forgot the LORD their God, and He sold them into the hand of Sisera, commander of the army of Hazor, and into the hand of the Philistines, and into the hand of the king of Moab — and they fought against them.

They cried out to the LORD and said, 'We have sinned, because we have forsaken the LORD and have worshiped the Baals and the Ashtoreths. And now, may You deliver us from the hand of our enemies, and we will serve You.'
And the LORD sent Jerubbaal and Bedan and Jephthah and Samuel, and He delivered you from the hand of your enemies all around, and you dwelt in safety.
And you saw that Nahash, king of the Ammonites, was coming up against you, and you said to me, 'No — a king must rule over us' — though the LORD your God was already your king.
And now, behold the king whom you have chosen, whom you have requested. And behold, the LORD has set a king over you.
If you fear the LORD and serve Him and listen to His voice and do not rebel against the mouth of the LORD, and you, together with the king who rules over you, will follow the LORD your God.
But if you do not listen to the voice of the LORD and rebel against the mouth of the LORD, the hand of the LORD will be against you, as it was against your ancestors.
Even now, take your stand and see this great thing that the LORD will do before you.

Is today not the wheat harvest? I will call to the LORD, and He will give a thunderstorm and rain, so that you will know and see that the evil you have committed in the eyes of the LORD is great, by asking for a king for yourselves.
And Samuel called to the LORD, and on that day the LORD gave a thunderstorm and rain, and all the people greatly feared the LORD and Samuel.
All the people said to Samuel, 'Pray for your servants to the LORD your God, that we may not die — for we have added evil to all our sins, namely asking for a king for ourselves.'
Samuel said to the people, 'Do not fear. You have indeed committed all this evil, but do not turn aside from following the LORD, and serve the LORD with all your heart.
And do not turn aside, for going after empty things — which do not profit and do not deliver — for those things are empty.
For the LORD will not forsake His people, for the sake of His great name, for the LORD was pleased to make you His people.
And as for me, may it be far from me to sin against the LORD by ceasing to pray for you. And I will teach you in the good and upright way.
Only fear the LORD and serve Him in truth, with all your heart — for consider what He has done greatly with you.
But if you continue to do evil — both you and your king will be swept away.'
